references.title: Stardew Valley Expanded CP package structure sample
notes: Used as a structural reference only. Do not copy story text, dialogue, art assets, map files, or unique creative content into generated projects.
The package uses JSONC-style files with comments and trailing commas, so future import/reference tooling should parse Content Patcher files leniently instead of strict JSON only.
The top-level content.json acts as an orchestration file and delegates major feature areas through Include patches.

package_layout_patterns.description: Keep content.json focused on Format, ConfigSchema, and Include entries for large feature areas.
Group included patch files by domain such as NPCs, Items, Locations, Shops, Festivals, Other, and OtherEvents.

Group assets by purpose, e.g. CharacterFiles/Portraits, CharacterFiles/OverworldSprites, CharacterFiles/Schedules, CharacterFiles/Dialogue, Maps/Locations, Maps/MapPatches, Items, Buildings, and Trees.
Use {{i18n:key}} for player-facing names and descriptions in item, NPC, map, event, and dialogue-related data.
Expose user-facing config keys in ConfigSchema, then gate optional patches with When conditions that reference those config tokens.
Use TextOperations for appending/removing small pieces from vanilla strings or delimited data, instead of replacing large vanilla records when possible.

ai_guidance: For small mods, generating a single content.json is acceptable. For multi-feature mods, recommend Include files grouped by feature area.
When the user asks for a character, suggest a bundle: Data/Characters, dialogue asset, schedule asset, gift tastes, portrait/sprite assets, optional spouse/roommate content, and optional events.
When the user asks for many items, suggest i18n keys, shared texture path conventions, object fields, shop entries, gift taste edits, recipes, and mail rewards as separate todo steps.
When generating conditions, prefer common CP When tokens like Season, Day, DayOfWeek, Time, Weather, HasSeenEvent, HasFlag, HasMod, Relationship, Hearts, Spouse, Roommate, FarmType, and config keys.
Use TextOperations for incremental edits to existing vanilla data when replacing an entire record would be fragile.
Keep generated custom IDs namespaced and stable, using the manifest UniqueID or author prefix.
